concerns.text: Acronym 'LVLM' (Large Vision-Language Model) is used in the Abstract and Introduction without being spelled out at first use. Define it as 'large vision-language models (LVLMs)' on first occurrence.

The term 'LongPT' is introduced in the Introduction as an abbreviation for 'long-context continued pre-training' but is not consistently defined at the very first mention in the Abstract or early Introduction. Ensure the full phrase precedes the acronym on first use.
The acronym 'SFT' (Supervised Fine-Tuning) appears in Section 4 (Comparing Long-Document VQA...) and Section 5 without prior definition. Spell out 'supervised fine-tuning (SFT)' at first use.
The term 'mRoPE' (modified Rotary Positional Embedding) is used in Section 3 and the Appendix without definition. Define it as 'modified Rotary Positional Embedding (mRoPE)' upon first introduction.
The acronym 'FSDP' (Fully Sharded Data Parallel) is used in the Appendix (Implementation Details) without definition. Define it as 'Fully Sharded Data Parallel (FSDP)' at first use.
The term 'DPI' (Dots Per Inch) is used in Section 4.1. While common, in a formal paper it should be defined as 'dots per inch (DPI)' on first use to ensure accessibility for non-specialist readers.

The phrase 'needle-in-a-haystack' is used as a task name (MM-NIAH) without a brief explanatory clause for general readers. Consider adding 'a retrieval task where a specific piece of information must be found within a large context' upon first mention.
